title: Ceropegia malwanensis
intro: Ceropegia malwanensis, commonly known as Malwan rosy pink vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia malwanensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit potential therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Ceropegia malwanensis is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Ceropegia malwanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Ceropegia, which is a group of plants commonly known as rope vines or carousel flowers. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, including milkweeds, oleanders, and periwinkles. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes the families Rubiaceae and Loganiaceae. Ceropegia malwanensis is native to India, where it can be found in dry, rocky areas.
